entry_rationale: Outcome Status -- STRONGLY FAVORED: the S&P 500 closed at 6,775.80 on March 11, 2026, leaving 300.80 index points of cushion above the 6,475 cutoff going into the March 13, 2026 close.  Supporting Evidence -- the contract wins on NO unless the index falls 4.4393% or more from that close within roughly 45.5 hours, which is a larger required move than the narrower S&P band markets in this universe and makes this the safest remaining non-excluded reference-price candidate.  Resolution Source -- Kalshi's underlying reference is the official S&P 500 index value published by S&P Dow Jones Indices, whose methodology states index levels are available through S&P DJI's website and major quote vendors; Risk Checklist -- passed clear resolution source, no formal appeals/reviews, strong position from a 300.80-point cushion, and recent corroboration from a same-day close.  Risk Level -- LOW; this was selected as the single lowest-risk available candidate after eliminating Tier 1 crypto and Tier 2 speaking markets and deprioritizing Tier 3 weather markets under the scan rules. Remaining Risks -- a sharp two-session equity selloff driven by escalation in the Iran war or tariff headlines could still push the index below 6,475, but no formal challenge path exists because settlement is mechanical; Sources -- https://za.investing.com/news/economy-news/wall-st-futures-subdued-as-investors-eye-crude-prices-inflation-report-4158008 ; https://www.spglobal.com/spdji/en/documents/methodologies/methodology-sp-us-indices.pdf.
